location.climate_notes: Early June peak growing season; long days, warming soil, morning sun, typical humidity

size_estimates.method: 12 clockwise photos at face level + 3-4 overhead; camera height ~173 cm for 5'9" photographer; garden markers (2-3 ft cage, 5-6 ft shepherd's hook)

root_system.notes: Dense fibrous roots from container transplant; focus watering and feeding within ~3 ft of base

light_pattern.pruning_implication: Prioritize tip-pinching tallest eastern stems; pinch inner and west/south shoots for balance

photo_protocol.walk_pattern: Clockwise around plant at consistent height
photo_protocol.overhead_shots: Last 3-4 photos from above for top foliage

companion_plants.base_seedlings: Likely rutabaga or other brassica volunteers
companion_plants.impact: Minor competition; rutabaga will bolt in June heat and decline by mid-summer
companion_plants.recommendation: Optional to pull while small; dying brassica adds minor organic matter

peak_growth.strength: Half to full label strength while watering

flowering.formula: Bloom booster higher in P and K (5-10-10 or 10-30-20)

pruning_guidance.technique: Tip-pinch or lightly prune soft green growing tips (1-3 inches) above leaf nodes
pruning_guidance.science: Removes apical meristem; reduces auxin apical dominance; awakens lateral buds
pruning_guidance.timing: Early to mid-morning on mild days; every 2-4 weeks during active growth
current_priority: Remove damaged, holey, or yellowing lower leaves
Tip-pinch tallest eastern stems to counter lean
Pinch inner and west/south-facing shoots for bushiness
Thin weak overcrowded basal shoots if needed

actions_completed.result: New side shoots emerging within days; successful apical dominance break

pest_monitoring.treatment: Hose off, insecticidal soap, or neem oil in evening

winter_notes.roots: Overwinter fine with mulch in mild winters

constraints: Hand injury (pins/cast) — prefer finger-pinching over heavy tool use
